turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <radialGradient id="bgGrad" cx="50%" cy="40%" r="80%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#070617" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#0b0f1a" />
    </radialGradient>
    <radialGradient id="marsGrad" cx="35%" cy="30%" r="70%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ffd6bf" />
      <stop offset="35%" stop-color="#ff7a45" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#9b2a18" />
    </radialGradient>
    <clipPath id="planetClip">
      <circle cx="300" cy="260" r="170" />
    </clipPath>
    <filter id="softGlow" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="8" result="b" />
      <feMerge><feMergeNode in="b"/><feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/></feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="url(#bgGrad)" />

  <!-- stars -->
  <g fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.85">
    <circle cx="40" cy="60" r="1.8" opacity="0.9" />
    <circle cx="120" cy="40" r="1.2" opacity="0.7" />
    <circle cx="200" cy="90" r="2.0" opacity="0.85" />
    <circle cx="460" cy="30" r="1.4" opacity="0.6" />
    <circle cx="360" cy="120" r="1.6" opacity="0.8" />
    <circle cx="80" cy="210" r="1.1" opacity="0.6" />
    <circle cx="280" cy="30" r="1.3" opacity="0.7" />
    <circle cx="450" cy="200" r="1.6" opacity="0.75" />
    <circle cx="330" cy="420" r="1.2" opacity="0.6" />
    <circle cx="150" cy="330" r="1.4" opacity="0.7" />
  </g>

  <!-- planet (Mars) -->
  <g filter="url(#softGlow)">
    <circle cx="300" cy="260" r="170" fill="url(#marsGrad)" />
    <!-- subtle highlight -->
    <ellipse cx="235" cy="200" rx="68" ry="68" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.12" />
  </g>

  <!-- surface details clipped to planet -->
  <g clip-path="url(#planetClip)">
    <!-- darker regions / plains -->
    <path d="M210 170 C250 150, 320 160, 360 190 C340 230, 280 240, 240 230 C210 220, 200 200, 210 170 Z" fill="#7a2a1d" opacity="0.7" />
    <ellipse cx="330" cy="300" rx="90" ry="50" fill="#7b2b1f" opacity="0.55" transform="rotate(-18 330 300)" />
    <ellipse cx="260" cy="330" rx="40" ry="26" fill="#8b3a2b" opacity="0.5" />

    <!-- craters -->
    <g opacity="0.9">
      <circle cx="320" cy="240" r="22" fill="#8f3b2a" />
      <circle cx="320" cy="240" r="10" fill="#b55a44" opacity="0.12" />

      <circle cx="220" cy="300" r="28" fill="#8a3428" />
      <circle cx="220" cy="300" r="12" fill="#c46b53" opacity="0.12" />

      <circle cx="280" cy="200" r="16" fill="#8e3a2a" />
      <circle cx="280" cy="200" r="7" fill="#c76b57" opacity="0.12" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- two small moons -->
  <g>
    <g transform="translate(420,110)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="18" fill="#cfcfcf" />
      <circle cx="-6" cy="-3" r="4" fill="#b3b3b3" />
      <circle cx="5" cy="5" r="3" fill="#b7b7b7" />
    </g>

    <g transform="translate(370,70)">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="12" ry="10" fill="#d3d3d3" />
      <circle cx="-4" cy="2" r="2.5" fill="#b9b9b9" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- faint planetary ring-like dust (subtle) -->
  <ellipse cx="300" cy="260" rx="220" ry="80" fill="none" stroke="#ffb8a0" stroke-opacity="0.06" stroke-width="18" />
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <radialGradient id="bgGrad" cx="50%" cy="40%" r="80%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#070617" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#0b0f1a" />
    </radialGradient>
    <radialGradient id="marsGrad" cx="35%" cy="30%" r="70%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ffd6bf" />
      <stop offset="35%" stop-color="#ff7a45" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#9b2a18" />
    </radialGradient>
    <clipPath id="planetClip">
      <circle cx="300" cy="260" r="170" />
    </clipPath>
    <filter id="softGlow" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="8" result="b" />
      <feMerge><feMergeNode in="b"/><feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/></feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="url(#bgGrad)" />

  <!-- stars -->
  <g fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.85">
    <circle cx="40" cy="60" r="1.8" opacity="0.9" />
    <circle cx="120" cy="40" r="1.2" opacity="0.7" />
    <circle cx="200" cy="90" r="2.0" opacity="0.85" />
    <circle cx="460" cy="30" r="1.4" opacity="0.6" />
    <circle cx="360" cy="120" r="1.6" opacity="0.8" />
    <circle cx="80" cy="210" r="1.1" opacity="0.6" />
    <circle cx="280" cy="30" r="1.3" opacity="0.7" />
    <circle cx="450" cy="200" r="1.6" opacity="0.75" />
    <circle cx="330" cy="420" r="1.2" opacity="0.6" />
    <circle cx="150" cy="330" r="1.4" opacity="0.7" />
  </g>

  <!-- planet (Mars) -->
  <g filter="url(#softGlow)">
    <circle cx="300" cy="260" r="170" fill="url(#marsGrad)" />
    <!-- subtle highlight -->
    <ellipse cx="235" cy="200" rx="68" ry="68" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.12" />
  </g>

  <!-- surface details clipped to planet -->
  <g clip-path="url(#planetClip)">
    <!-- darker regions / plains -->
    <path d="M210 170 C250 150, 320 160, 360 190 C340 230, 280 240, 240 230 C210 220, 200 200, 210 170 Z" fill="#7a2a1d" opacity="0.7" />
    <ellipse cx="330" cy="300" rx="90" ry="50" fill="#7b2b1f" opacity="0.55" transform="rotate(-18 330 300)" />
    <ellipse cx="260" cy="330" rx="40" ry="26" fill="#8b3a2b" opacity="0.5" />

    <!-- craters -->
    <g opacity="0.9">
      <circle cx="320" cy="240" r="22" fill="#8f3b2a" />
      <circle cx="320" cy="240" r="10" fill="#b55a44" opacity="0.12" />

      <circle cx="220" cy="300" r="28" fill="#8a3428" />
      <circle cx="220" cy="300" r="12" fill="#c46b53" opacity="0.12" />

      <circle cx="280" cy="200" r="16" fill="#8e3a2a" />
      <circle cx="280" cy="200" r="7" fill="#c76b57" opacity="0.12" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- two small moons -->
  <g>
    <g transform="translate(420,110)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="18" fill="#cfcfcf" />
      <circle cx="-6" cy="-3" r="4" fill="#b3b3b3" />
      <circle cx="5" cy="5" r="3" fill="#b7b7b7" />
    </g>

    <g transform="translate(370,70)">
      <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="12" ry="10" fill="#d3d3d3" />
      <circle cx="-4" cy="2" r="2.5" fill="#b9b9b9" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- faint planetary ring-like dust (subtle) -->
  <ellipse cx="300" cy="260" rx="220" ry="80" fill="none" stroke="#ffb8a0" stroke-opacity="0.06" stroke-width="18" />
</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Creating SVG for Mars**

I’m thinking about how to create an SVG image that represents the word "Mars" without using any letters or numbers. I'll depict the reddish-orange planet with gradients and surface features, like darker patches for maria and maybe some subtle craters. I could also add two small gray moons, representing Phobos and Deimos, near the planet. Everything needs to fit within a 512x512 canvas, all encapsulated in a proper JSON format. I need to ensure I escape quotes correctly!**Developing SVG for Mars Representation**

I’m working on the SVG content, aiming for a 512x512 design that captures the essence of Mars within a starry background. I计划 to use a dark radial gradient for the starfield and sprinkle in some small white and yellow stars. The planet itself will be a large circle featuring a radial gradient transitioning from vibrant orange to deep red. I’ll add darker irregular shapes for surface features and some craters, ensuring to keep everything simple. I might utilize a highlight overlay for added depth as well!**Refining SVG Elements for Mars**

I’m thinking about how to improve my Mars SVG design by simplifying the highlight. Instead of blending, I might just add a smaller, offset circle with some opacity. I’ll include two small gray moons with irregular shapes and crater details near the top left. Everything must remain text-free and valid as SVG. 

I’ll define gradients and use a clip path for the planet, then create the starry background using small circles for stars. The features will include dark patches and craters inside the planet!**Finalizing SVG and JSON Structure**

I need to make sure not to include any text or numbers as characters in my SVG drawing—only attributes and coordinates are acceptable. This means I can return a JSON object with one field for the SVG string, but I'll have to escape quotes properly. 

When I construct the JSON, I'll use double quotes for the string and escape the quotes within the SVG. I’m aiming for about 35 to 45 lines of SVG content, so I’ll prepare that now and get everything ready for the final JSON output!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

message.reasoning:
**Generating space object words**

I'm brainstorming plausible single-word space object names inspired by celestial themes. I should stick to recognizable terms that avoid awkward constructions. Starting with "mars" as my top choice, then moving to "planet," and next "redplanet." After that, "exoplanet" feels fitting too. I might include "saturn" for its famous rings and "jupiter," known for the red spot. "Moon" could work, then I think about "orb," "sphere," and "planetary." Keeping it simple and avoiding hyphens or spaces helps!**Compiling a list of nouns**

I’m putting together a list of 20 common nouns related to space objects. I’ll start with "mars," "planet," and "redplanet," followed by "exoplanet," "saturn," and "jupiter." I’ll add "venus," "mercury," "neptune," and "uranus" for a well-rounded selection. Then I’ll think about "moon," "orb," "sphere," and "asteroid." I might include "comet" and "celestial," too, though "celestial" is technically an adjective. I need to ensure it’s all lower-case and do my best to avoid any repeats for clarity!
